title: Comma Sense: National Punctuation Day!
story: Writers, teachers, and grammar lovers celebrate National Punctuation Day every September 24 by sharing their favorite punctuation marks! People post funny memes about commas, semicolons, and exclamation points on social media. Some schools hold contests where kids create stories using as many different punctuation marks as possible.
activity_question: What do people share on social media during National Punctuation Day?
follow_up_question: Which punctuation mark do you think deserves the most appreciation?

title: Flaming Fruit Fun Day
story: Chefs light desserts on fire every September 24 for National Cherries Jubilee Day! This fancy dessert starts with sweet cherries cooked in syrup, then gets a splash of brandy or rum that's set ablaze. The blue flames dance over the fruit before being poured over vanilla ice cream. Restaurants love showing off this dramatic dessert, and home cooks post videos of their flaming creations online. It's the perfect mix of delicious and exciting!
activity_question: What happens to the brandy or rum in cherries jubilee?
bonus_fact: The dessert was created for Queen Victoria's Golden Jubilee celebration in 1887.

title: Rainbow Nation Celebration Day
story: South Africans throw the ultimate cultural party every September 24 for Heritage Day! Families fire up braais (barbecues) in their backyards, cooking boerewors sausages and sharing traditional recipes passed down through generations. People wear colorful traditional clothing from their different cultures - Zulu beadwork, Afrikaner bonnets, Indian saris, and Xhosa blankets. Schools host festivals where kids learn dances, songs, and stories from all eleven official languages. It's like having eleven different birthday parties rolled into one amazing day!
activity_question: What do South African families traditionally cook during Heritage Day celebrations?
bonus_fact: South Africa has eleven official languages - more than almost any other country!

title: Schwenkfelder Thanksgiving: Double the Gratitude!
story: The Schwenkfelder community celebrates their own special Thanksgiving on September 24 each year! This unique tradition started when Schwenkfelder families from Germany wanted to give extra thanks for their safe journey to America. Today, Schwenkfelder churches hold special services with prayers, hymns, and community meals. Families gather to share traditional foods and reflect on their blessings, just like regular Thanksgiving but with their own twist!
activity_question: What makes Schwenkfelder Thanksgiving different from regular Thanksgiving?
activity_choices: It's celebrated by a specific religious community
It lasts for three whole days
activity_answer: It's celebrated by a specific religious community
follow_up_question: What special traditions does your family have for giving thanks?
